title: Noga-ta

'''Noga-ta''' was an [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and became the legendary "'''Lost Jedi'''" on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Barab I/Legends|Barab I]] after having prevented warfare from engulfing the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]].

==Biography==
Noga-ta, a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]], was a member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] before the [[Declaration of a New Order|fall]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Although accounts differed, the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] Noga-ta arrived on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Barab I/Legends|Barab I]] just as the [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Barabel/Legends|Barabels]] were about to erupt into warfare. Two large factions had begun to fight over a hunting territory, and were readying themselves for war. Noga-ta created a peace treaty that ended the conflict before it began, potentially saving countless lives. The Jedi's prescriptions for peace—including giving a tribe's largest kill of the night to a neighboring tribe—were still enforced centuries later. Noga-ta's service on Barab I became legend, with the story of the "great warrior from beyond the clouds" being passed down from generation to generation through the use of cave paintings and oral recitations. Forever after, Barabel held all Jedi in the highest regard, and because of Noga-ta's actions, they always heeded the word of a Jedi.<ref name="GORW" />

==Behind the scenes==
===Conception===
Noga-ta was first mentioned in the sourcebook ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]''. Noga-ta was illustrated by [[Jeremy Jarvis]] in the book, which was published in [[2004]] by [[Wizards of the Coast]].<ref name="GORW" />

===LEGO ''Star Wars''===
Noga-ta was voiced by [[Eric Bauza]] in various non-[[canon]]ical [[LEGO Star Wars|LEGO ''Star Wars'']] releases, including the ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles]]'' episode "[[Escape from the Jedi Temple]]." In the episode, Noga-ta traveled to [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to assist [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] mission to recover [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]] from the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] after the [[Order 66/Legends|initiation]] of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]]. Nicknamed "'''Rusty'''," Noga-ta's reunion with his old teacher and former roommate is cut short, however, when he realizes that neither Jedi remembered him.<ref name="Escape">{{YodaChronicles|Escape from the Jedi Temple}}</ref>

A [[LEGO]] minifigure of Noga-ta was released in the set "[[W:c:lego:75051 Jedi Scout Fighter|Jedi Scout Fighter]]," where the Jedi is simply named "Ithorian Jedi Master" and serves as Jek-14's co-pilot.<ref name="Scout Fighter">{{LEGOCite|num=75051|name=Jedi Scout Fighter}}</ref> Whereas Noga-ta has a blue-bladed lightsaber and does not fly in the starship in "Escape from the Jedi Temple,"<ref name="Escape" /> the Ithorian's minifigure in "Jedi Scout Fighter" holds a green-bladed lightsaber.<ref name="Scout Fighter" /> Noga-ta is also depicted with a green blade in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[LEGO Star Wars: The New Yoda Chronicles|''LEGO Star Wars The New Yoda Chronicles'']]. The player could play as Noga-ta in a level of the game in which he free-falls through a subterranean sinkhole filled with various platforms and wreckages of [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|Republic gunships]]; [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1 battle droids]] and [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] fire upon Noga-ta with their blasters while he collects holocrons.<ref name="NYC game">''[[LEGO Star Wars: The New Yoda Chronicles]]''</ref>
